basic_details.job_title: UPSSSC Lower PCS Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 2516 Clerk, Executive Officer and More Posts
basic_details.department: Uttar Pradesh Subordinate Services Selection Commission (UPSSSC), Lucknow
basic_details.post_name: Multiple Posts across Departments (See Vacancy Table)
basic_details.total_vacancies: 2516 Posts (Revised from 2285 via Corrigendum dated 29 May 2026)
basic_details.short_information: Important Note: Only those candidates who appeared in PET-2025 (Preliminary Eligibility Test-2025) and have been issued a Score Card by UPSSSC are eligible to apply for this Main Examination. Candidates with zero or negative normalised/absolute scores in PET-2025 will not be shortlisted.

application_fee.details: Note: Fee can be paid via Credit Card, Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or SBI e-Challan only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Candidates must reconcile the fee and submit the final application before 18 June 2026. Application amendment is allowed until 25 June 2026.

age_limit.age_details: 21 to 40 years (post-wise, as on 1st July of the recruitment year)

qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor's Degree from a recognised university (Graduate Level)
qualification.details: Educational Qualification (Post-wise): | Assistant Treasury Accountant (Directorate of Treasury): Bachelor's Degree from a university established by law in India or equivalent qualification recognised by the Government, as per UP Treasury Accountant (Non-Gazetted) Service Rules, 2019, Part-4, Rule-8. | Account Inspector (Cooperative Societies & Panchayat): Bachelor's Degree from a university established by law in India, as per UP Subordinate (Cooperative Societies and Panchayat) Accounts Audit Service Rules, 2025, Part-4, Rule-8. | Amin/Auctioneer (State Agriculture Mandi Parishad): Bachelor's Degree from any recognised university, as per UP Agriculture Production Mandi Samiti (Centralised) Service Rules, 1984 as amended, Rule-7. | Executive Officer (Municipal Administration): Bachelor's Degree from a recognised institution as per UP Municipal Centralised Service Rules, 1966 as amended. Additionally, a Computer Diploma ("O" level or above) from a recognised institution is required at the time of regularisation for direct recruits. | Clerk-cum-Typist / Cashier-cum-Clerk / Account Clerk (Agriculture Mandi Parishad): Bachelor's Degree from any recognised university, as per UP Agriculture Production Mandi Samiti (Centralised) Service Rules, 1984 as amended, Rule-7. | Assistant Consolidation Officer / Assistant Revenue Officer (Office of Consolidation Commissioner): Bachelor's Degree from a university established by law in India, as per UP Land Consolidation Service Rules, 1992 as amended, Part-4, Rule-8. Candidates with a Law Degree (LLB) from any university will also get preference in direct recruitment. | Assistant Promoter (Non-Technical) - General/Special Selection (Industries & Enterprise Promotion Directorate): Bachelor's Degree or equivalent qualification recognised by the Government, as per UP Industry Department Subordinate Service Rules, 1992, Part-4, Rule-8. | Centre In-Charge (UP State Road Transport Corporation): Bachelor's Degree from a university established by law in India or equivalent qualification recognised by the State Government, along with working knowledge of Hindi in Devanagari script, as per UP State Road Transport Corporation Service Rules, 1981 as amended, Part-4, Rule-18. | District Assistant Training Officer (Family Welfare Mahadirectorate): Candidates must have passed the Intermediate Board Examination of UP with Biology and Mathematics subjects and hold a Bachelor's Degree from any recognised university, as per Government Order No. 2859/5-10-2004-JI-137/2004 dated 10 September 2004. | Mandi Inspector Grade-2 (State Agriculture Mandi Parishad): Bachelor's Degree with at least 50% marks and Intermediate with at least 50% marks from a recognised university (for General/Unreserved candidates). SC/ST/OBC candidates need at least 45% in both Graduation and Intermediate. | Preferential Qualification (All Posts): Preference will be given to candidates who have served in the Provincial Armed Forces (Pradeshik Sena) for a minimum of 2 years, or who have obtained the 'B' Certificate of the National Cadet Corps (NCC), all other conditions being equal. | Age Limit: Generally 21 to 40 years as on 1st July of the year in which recruitment is advertised. Age relaxation is applicable for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-Servicemen, Freedom Fighter Dependants, PwBD, Women, Outstanding Sportspersons and Departmental Candidates as per UP Government rules and the specific service rules applicable to each post. | Note: For EWS category candidates, the EWS certificate must be issued between 01 April 2026 and 18 June 2026 (last date of application), based on income of financial year 2025-26 and valid for financial year 2026-27. The certificate must be in the prescribed format as per UP Government Office Memorandum No. 3/2019/4/1/2002/ka-2/19TCI.SI.-II dated 14 March 2019.

The UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services (Graduate Level) Recruitment 2026 selection will be based on a Written Main Examination (Likhit Pariksha).

2 Hours): There will be one question paper containing 100 objective-type multiple-choice questions carrying 100 marks with a duration of 2 hours (120 minutes). There is negative marking of 1/4 (25%) of prescribed marks for each wrong answer.
The examination paper will be divided into three parts as follows:
Shortlisting for Main Exam: Candidates will be shortlisted for the Main Examination based on their normalised score in PET-2025
taking 15 times the category-wise vacancies (up to the final cut-off score
how_to_apply: Eligible candidates for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services Recruitment 2026 must apply online through the official UPSSSC portal before the last date.

Step 1 - Login via PET-2025 Registration Number: Visit upsssc.gov.in and login using your PET-2025 Registration Number and OTP sent to the registered mobile number/email. Only PET-2025 registered candidates can apply.

Step 2 - Select Posts and Fill Application: Your personal details from PET-2025 will be auto-displayed. Select the post(s) you wish to apply for, verify eligibility, and fill in all educational qualification, community, and other details carefully.
Step 3 - Enter Post Preference: In the "Other Details" section, enter your post and department preference priority and indicate preferential/additional qualification details (if applicable).
Step 4 - Verify Photo and Signature: Your photo and signature uploaded in PET-2025 will be auto-displayed. No changes can be made. Click Continue to proceed.

Step 5 - Pay Application Fee: Pay Rs.25/- via Credit Card, Debit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or SBI e-Challan. After successful payment, your application form will be auto-submitted. Download and print the application form immediately.

Step 6 - Fee Reconciliation & Amendment: If required, reconcile fee and amend permissible details (correspondence address, EWS/horizontal reservation category, educational qualification, marital status) on the Applicant's Dashboard by 25 June 2026. Save a printout of the final application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services (Graduate Level) Recruitment 2026 is a state-level mains examination conducted by Uttar Pradesh Subordinate Services Selection Commission (UPSSSC) to fill 2516 Group C posts across 11 departments in Uttar Pradesh.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 recruitment?
faq.answer: UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 notification (Advt. No. 07-Exam/2026) has been released for a total of 2516 vacancies for various posts such as Assistant Treasury Accountant, Account Inspector, Amin/Auctioneer, Executive Officer, Clerk-cum-Typist, Assistant Consolidation Officer and other posts.

faq.question: Q3. What are the online application dates for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services 2026?
faq.answer: The online application form for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services (Lower PCS) 2026 will be available from 29 May 2026 to 18 June 2026 on the official website upsssc.gov.in.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility criteria for UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 exam?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Bachelor’s Degree (Graduate Level) from a recognised university, and only those who appeared in UPSSSC PET 2025 and hold a valid PET-2025 score card will be eligible to apply for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services Main Examination 2026.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age for UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 is 21 years and the maximum age is 40 years, with upper-age relaxation applicable for reserved categories as per Uttar Pradesh Government rules.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 form?
faq.answer: The application fee for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services (Graduate Level) Recruitment 2026 is Rs. 25 (online processing fee only) for all categories, which can be paid via Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, UPI or SBI e-Challan.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process for UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 includes shortlisting of candidates based on UPSSSC PET 2025 normalised score followed by a single mains written examination of 100 objective-type questions and document verification.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern of UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 mains exam?
faq.answer: UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services Mains Exam 2026 consists of one paper with 100 multiple-choice questions for 100 marks, a duration of 2 hours, and a negative marking of 1/4 mark for each wrong answer; questions are asked from General Studies, General Hindi, Computer & IT, and Uttar Pradesh General Knowledge.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary for UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services posts in 2026?
faq.answer: Selected candidates under UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 will get pay scales ranging from Level-4 (Rs. 25,500–81,100) to Level-6 (Rs. 35,400–1,12,400) depending on the post, as per the respective service rules of different departments in Uttar Pradesh.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I download the UPSSSC Lower PCS 2026 Notification PDF and apply online?
faq.answer: Candidates can download the official UPSSSC Combined Lower Subordinate Services (Graduate Level) Notification 2026 PDF and apply online only from the official website of Uttar Pradesh Subordinate Services Selection Commission at upsssc.gov.in within the specified dates.
